A few little notes on the subxivoid port.
I always put CO2 in the chest first, which really pushes the diaphragm down.
So when you get into the chest, you are going to be miles above the diaphragm and you just never go below the diaphragm, especially, well, entirely because you've got CO2 in the chest.
And Bob Sofoglio's technique would always use CO2 in the chest for robotics anyway, although Bernie Park doesn't use it.
